schemas.headline: S&P 500 Stock Screener Tool
schemas.description: Free interactive stock screener for the S&P 500. Filter stocks by P/E ratio, dividend yield, market cap, sector, beta, ROE, debt, and revenue growth.

acceptedAnswer.text: A stock screener is a tool that allows investors to filter through thousands of stocks based on specific criteria like market capitalization, price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio, dividend yield, and sector.

mainEntity.name: How do you screen stocks by dividend yield?

acceptedAnswer.text: To screen stocks by dividend yield, set a minimum percentage threshold in the screener's dividend yield filter. For example, setting the minimum to 3% will only show stocks that pay a 3% or higher annualized dividend relative to their current share price.

mainEntity.name: What is a good P/E ratio for screening stocks?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 'good' P/E ratio depends on the industry, but generally, value investors look for P/E ratios under 15-20. Growth stocks may have much higher P/E ratios because investors are willing to pay a premium for future earnings growth.

Our S&P 500 stock screener comes pre-loaded with companies from the index. You can further narrow down the list by adjusting the sliders for metrics like P/E, beta, and revenue growth, or by selecting specific sectors like Technology or Healthcare.

mainEntity.name: Is this S&P 500 stock screener free?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, our interactive stock screener is completely free to use and provides real-time or end-of-day data for fundamental metrics without requiring a subscription.
